body.title: Inference router increment 4 — LIVE re-measurement so time_buckets never go stale
body.intention: I intend that a source's time_bucket reflects its CURRENT speed, so a box that got faster (Hex) is promoted automatically and a box that degraded is demoted — buckets are live, not baked.
body.description:
**Problem — this is the headline principle, and increments 1-3 don't deliver it:** the whole design turns on time being LIVE ('Hex collapsed a bucket — same job, same dollars, now interactive instead of overnight'). Increments 1-3 add a `time_bucket` FIELD and populate it ONCE. That is a static label with finer granularity — the exact 'stale ollama=slow label exiles Hex' failure the epic exists to kill. Difficulty has a corrector (failure-bump); a wrong `time_bucket` has NONE and silently misroutes with no feedback path. This increment builds that feedback path. GATED on T-ds-local-ollama-route (needs at least one real source registered to re-measure).
**Proposed shape:** a live resource-read that (1) records observed wall-clock latency per source from the crossing-log (increment 1 already logs it) and/or a lightweight periodic probe, (2) recomputes each source's `time_bucket` from a rolling window (thresholds: interactive-seconds / minutes / overnight), (3) writes the updated bucket back where the selector reads it, and (4) logs every bucket TRANSITION (source X: overnight->interactive) as a state change. A source that goes unreachable drops out (already handled by availability + system_alarm); this ticket handles the SPEED dimension, not up/down.
**Scope note (load, not just speed):** consequence-effect #6 (Hex overloaded-but-up still always wins because dollars=0) is the SAME root gap — a busy source's latency rises, so re-measurement demotes its time_bucket and the selector naturally sheds load. No separate load-shedder needed IF re-measurement is responsive; tune the window for that.
**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/ (a resource-read/metrics component + the write-back into the source descriptors; likely sources.py + a small monitor in the inference device). Reads the crossing-log; NO SQLite (flat-file / in-memory rolling window + Postgres only if shared).
**Design rules:** ALL inference through the Proxy; NO SQLite; log every bucket transition (state change) and the read crossing.
**Scope boundary:** the SPEED re-measurement + write-back + transition logging ONLY. Up/down availability and system_alarm already exist (increment 2). Does NOT add a quality judge.
**Test plan (red->green):** (1) feed synthetic latencies below the interactive threshold -> source's time_bucket becomes interactive; feed slow latencies -> becomes overnight; (2) a bucket TRANSITION emits a state-change log line; (3) the selector, re-run after a re-measure, routes differently (a source promoted interactive now wins an interactive call it was excluded from before) — this is the Hex-collapsed-a-bucket scenario, proven; (4) rolling-window: one outlier does not flip the bucket (hysteresis).
**Completion criteria:** time_bucket is DERIVED from observed/probed latency on a rolling window, written back where the selector reads, with transitions logged; a promote/demote changes routing on the next call; a single outlier doesn't thrash the bucket.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: Live re-measurement shipped: resource_monitor.ResourceMonitor keeps a rolling latency window per source, maps the MEDIAN to a time_bucket (interactive<=10s/minutes<=300s/overnight) with hysteresis, writes back Source.time_bucket on change and logs the transition; device.py dispatch feeds every observed latency in. 8 tests green incl. promote/demote, hysteresis (outlier doesn't flip), transition logged, selector-reroutes-after-remeasure (the Hex-collapsed-a-bucket scenario), and a dispatch-wiring test. 59 inference tests green.
body.unproven_reason: proof_emitter requires the red state to fail on an ASSERTION, but for a NEW module the test-only red stub (cf0bb5ef) fails on ModuleNotFoundError (collection error), which it rejects. Correct pattern for a new module: commit a stub-MODULE first (symbols resolve, behavior wrong) so the red is an assertion, then impl. I committed test-only-stub then full impl. Mechanism fully proven by 8 green tests. Lever: adopt stub-module-first for new-module increments, OR a proof_emitter mode that accepts a symbol-stub as authentic red.
